assertions.text: Mentions calling pg_backend_pid() from inside the target psql session (not a transient one)
Recommends lldb on macOS over gdb (and explains the codesigning reason or at least flags it)
Gives the correct attach syntax: lldb -p <pid>
Names a real executor entry point as the breakpoint (ExecutorRun / ExecutorStart / exec_simple_query)
Warns that the postmaster is not the right process to attach to (per-connection fork model)
Uses paths consistent with the project (dev/install-debug/bin or similar) OR references /pg-attach
Mentions silencing SIGUSR1 in the debugger (latch wakeup noise)
Shows pprint(Node*) for plan inspection via expr/call
Mentions debug build with cassert enabled (so Asserts fire before the segfault)
Mentions enabling core dumps on macOS (ulimit -c unlimited and /cores)
Tells the user to open the core with lldb (lldb /path/to/postgres /cores/core.<pid>)
Suggests attaching lldb to the backend BEFORE the crash via pg_backend_pid()+lldb -p, or setting a breakpoint on errfinish/PG-level traps
Suggests elog/ereport instrumentation OR a pg_usleep waitpoint to catch fast paths
Mentions that postgres binary on macOS must not be hardened/Apple-signed for cores to land (or otherwise notes the macOS suppress-cores caveat)
Names errfinish specifically as the universal ereport breakpoint
Recommends single-user mode: postgres --single -D $PGDATA <dbname>
Explains that --single does not fork, so the whole lifetime is in one process the debugger can follow
Gives the correct invocation pattern: lldb -- postgres --single ... OR gdb --args postgres --single ...
Notes that single-user mode terminates commands on newline (not semicolon) OR mentions -j flag
Alternatively or additionally mentions PGOPTIONS="-W N" / pg_usleep waitpoint as a way to pause an early backend so you can attach
Distinguishes when to use --single vs -W vs spin-loop waitpoint (decision criteria)
Mentions that elog(LOG, ...) inside the waitpoint reveals the PID in the server log
